questions.q: Dholavira is a major site of which ancient civilization?
questions.explanation: Dholavira is one of the largest and best-preserved cities of the Indus Valley (Harappan) Civilization.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Dholavira located?
questions.explanation: Dholavira lies in the Kutch district of Gujarat in western India.

questions.q: Dholavira sits in which arid region of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Dholavira is located on Khadir island in the Great Rann of Kutch, a vast salt desert.

questions.q: Roughly how old is the Harappan city of Dholavira?
questions.explanation: Dholavira flourished as a Harappan city more than 4,500 years ago, with occupation beginning around 3000 BCE.

questions.q: What kind of engineering is Dholavira especially famous for?
questions.explanation: Dholavira is renowned for its advanced system of stone-cut water reservoirs and water-harvesting structures.

questions.q: Dholavira was inscribed in 2021 as a what?
questions.explanation: In 2021 Dholavira became a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the first Indus Valley site in India to gain that status.

questions.q: Which famous discovery at Dholavira is often called an ancient 'signboard'?
options: A row of large Indus script symbols found near a gateway
questions.explanation: A sequence of ten large Indus-script signs, believed to be a signboard, was found near the northern gateway of the citadel.

questions.q: What was the main building material used for Dholavira's structures?
questions.explanation: Unlike many Harappan sites built mostly of baked brick, Dholavira made extensive use of dressed stone alongside mud brick.

questions.q: Dholavira is one of the largest Harappan sites in which country?
questions.explanation: Dholavira, in Gujarat, is one of the five largest Harappan sites and among the most important Indus Valley cities within present-day India.

questions.q: What is the local name of the island on which Dholavira stands?
questions.explanation: Dholavira is situated on Khadir Bet (Khadir island) in the Rann of Kutch.

questions.q: Which archaeologist led the major excavations that revealed Dholavira's full layout?
questions.explanation: R. S. Bisht of the Archaeological Survey of India directed the extensive excavations at Dholavira from 1989 onward.

questions.q: How was Dholavira's city plan broadly divided?
options: Into a grid of identical square blocks only
Into three principal divisions: a citadel, a middle town, and a lower town
questions.explanation: Dholavira had a distinctive three-part plan comprising a citadel, a middle town, and a lower town.

questions.q: Two seasonal streams flanking Dholavira fed its reservoirs. What were they named?
questions.explanation: The seasonal streams Manhar and Mansar flanked the site and were dammed to channel water into Dholavira's reservoirs.

questions.q: Roughly what year did the site of Dholavira begin to be settled?
questions.explanation: Archaeology shows occupation at Dholavira beginning around 3000 BCE and continuing for well over a millennium.

questions.q: What did the city's builders cut directly into the bedrock to store water?
questions.explanation: Dholavirans excavated huge reservoirs into the rock to capture and store monsoon and stream water through the dry season.

questions.q: The large signboard inscription at Dholavira was written in which script?
questions.explanation: The signboard's ten symbols are in the Indus script, which remains undeciphered to this day.

questions.q: Which feature shows the careful defensive planning of Dholavira's citadel?
options: Massive fortification walls with elaborate gateways
questions.explanation: The citadel was enclosed by thick stone-and-brick fortification walls pierced by monumental, well-designed gateways.

questions.q: What open space at Dholavira lay between the citadel and the middle town?
options: A ceremonial ground or stadium-like open area
questions.explanation: A large open ground, often described as a ceremonial or stadium-like space, separated the citadel from the middle town.

questions.q: Approximately how many sign symbols make up the celebrated Dholavira 'signboard'?
questions.explanation: The signboard consists of ten large Indus-script signs, each originally inlaid in a wooden board.

questions.q: The Dholavira signboard's symbols were originally made of what inlaid material?
questions.explanation: The signs were formed from pieces of white gypsum set into a wooden plank that has since perished.

questions.q: Which materials suggest Dholavira was a center of craft and trade?
options: Beads, shell, and semi-precious stone artifacts
questions.explanation: Workshops and finds of beads, shell objects, and semi-precious stones point to Dholavira's role in craft production and trade.

questions.q: The Indus Valley Civilization, to which Dholavira belongs, was contemporary with which other ancient civilizations?
questions.explanation: The Harappan civilization flourished in the Bronze Age alongside ancient Mesopotamia and Egypt.

questions.q: In which year was Dholavira inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: Dholavira was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2021 as 'Dholavira: A Harappan City'.

questions.q: What is the modern Gujarati name of the village adjacent to the Dholavira ruins?
questions.explanation: The ruins lie beside the village of Dholavira and the mound is locally known as Kotada Timba.

questions.q: Dholavira is notable among Harappan cities for the unusually high proportion of which material in its construction?
questions.explanation: Dholavira stands out for the dominant use of dressed local stone, whereas sites like Harappa relied chiefly on baked brick.

questions.q: Excavators identified roughly how many water reservoirs encircling the city of Dholavira?
questions.explanation: Excavations revealed a series of reservoirs, numbering around sixteen, that ringed and supplied the fortified city.

questions.q: The decline of Dholavira around the early second millennium BCE is often linked to what?
options: Climate change, aridity, and the drying of water sources
questions.explanation: Dholavira's decline is associated with increasing aridity and the failure of its water supply during the broader Harappan collapse.

questions.q: Dholavira's design shows evidence of careful planning using which feature?
options: A standardized measurement and ratio system in its layout
questions.explanation: Scholars note that Dholavira's plan reflects deliberate proportional ratios and standardized units in its city design.

questions.q: Which other nearby Harappan sites in Gujarat are often studied alongside Dholavira?
questions.explanation: Lothal and Surkotada are other important Harappan sites in Gujarat frequently studied together with Dholavira.

questions.q: Which institution carried out the principal excavations and conservation of Dholavira?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) conducted the major excavations and continues to conserve the Dholavira site.
